title: I Am Allowed to Stop, Even If Not Everything Is Done
subtitle: An affirmation for unhooking from the need to finish everything before resting
excerpt: A short affirmation for Day 7 of the sleep arc. It names permission to stop as something already given — not a reward for completing the list, but a baseline available regardless of what got done.
sourceNotes: Day 7 theme from SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive to Fall Asleep Faster and Wake Energised (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798269649795).

practice.whenToUse: On an evening when you are still carrying the day's list — when resting feels like falling behind.

content.text: An affirmation for Day 7. It names stopping as a permission that exists before the list is finished — which is the only time it's actually useful.
The affirmation separates rest from completion. It names the truth that the list will never be fully done — and that stopping is available anyway.
I am allowed to stop, even if not everything is done. I have done enough for today. Tomorrow begins fresh.
Say it three times in the evening, preferably before getting into bed. The second sentence is the one that usually lands — let it stay for a breath.
(Rebecca J Page, 2025): unhooking from busyness. The affirmation is adapted from the Day 7 reframe lines.
